body:
**Severity**: MEDIUM (NPCs are visible after #772 close but pose like statues — visible-state gap, not functional-state)
**Source**: Live observation at `GSDocMitchellHouse` after #772 close (2026-05-03). Vanish bug fixed, AnimationPlayer attaches by default, but Doc Mitchell stands rigid — no chest rise/fall, no finger curl, no head settle.
**Related**: #772 (parent issue) — closed by parser-side FLT_MAX gate; this is the visible-motion follow-up.

## Summary

After [3c32a5e](https://github.com/matiaszanolli/ByroRedux/commit/3c32a5e), `AnimationPlayer::new(handle).with_root(skel_root)` attaches by default in `npc_spawn::spawn_npc_entity`. `mtidle.kf` parses cleanly with 56 channels — the #772 diagnostic dump confirmed every translation channel returns either `None` (correct bind fall-through for fingers/twists) or a value within ~1e-5 of bind-pose (real translation animation on spine, head, hands, feet, pelvis).

Yet the rendered NPC is visually static. mtidle is supposed to produce subtle but observable motion: chest rise/fall (~10 cm), gentle head settle, fingers slightly curled into a relaxed pose.

## Three suspect paths, in order of likelihood

1. **`AnimationPlayer.local_time` not advancing**. `advance_time(player, clip, dt)` is called once per tick at [systems.rs:371](byroredux/src/systems.rs#L371). If `dt` is zero on the NPC's player but non-zero elsewhere, or if the player gets removed somewhere, animation stays at frame 0 forever. Quickest probe: log `(local_time, prev_time)` for an NPC's player on every Nth tick.

2. **B-spline rotation sampling produces near-identity quaternions**. mtidle.kf binds 52 of 56 channels via `NiBSplineCompTransformInterpolator` (per #772 investigation). `extract_transform_channel_bspline` ([anim.rs:1340-1430](crates/nif/src/anim.rs#L1340-L1430)) dequantizes via `deboor_cubic` against `i16` control-point arrays with `(offset, half_range)` linear remap. If the dequantization has an off-by-one or a sign flip, every rotation sample lands near identity and the bone never moves. The unit-quaternion normalisation at lines 1369-1382 would silently mask near-zero magnitudes.

3. **Apply phase reads stale clip data**. The `AnimationClipRegistry` lookup at [systems.rs:368](byroredux/src/systems.rs#L368) is by handle; if the handle is wrong (e.g. all NPCs share handle 0 but the registry stored `mtidle.kf` under a different key), `registry.get(clip_handle)` returns `Some(empty_clip)` and the loop iterates zero channels. The diagnostic showed `channels=56` so this is unlikely, but worth ruling out by logging the resolved clip identity.

## Game impact

Goodsprings, Megaton, every interior cell with NPCs renders bodies in T-pose-like bind. Combat, NPC interaction, dialogue all blocked behind "NPCs need to react visibly".

## Suggested investigation order

1. Add a one-line log in [systems.rs:advance_time call](byroredux/src/systems.rs#L371) emitting `local_time` every 60 ticks per AnimationPlayer entity. Run for ~5 seconds. If `local_time` doesn't advance, suspect 1 is confirmed.
2. If `local_time` advances, sample one rotation channel (`bip01 spine` ideally) at `t=0.5` and `t=1.0`, log the quaternion. If both samples are within `~1e-3` of each other in any axis, the B-spline rot decoder is the culprit (suspect 2).
3. If rotations differ but apply phase isn't writing them, log `transform.rotation` post-apply on `bip01 spine`. If unchanged from bind, the apply phase has a bug.

## Locations

- [byroredux/src/systems.rs:340-560](byroredux/src/systems.rs#L340-L560) — animation_system (apply phase)
- [crates/nif/src/anim.rs:1259-1450](crates/nif/src/anim.rs#L1259-L1450) — extract_transform_channel_bspline (rotation B-spline path)
- [crates/core/src/animation/interpolation.rs](crates/core/src/animation/interpolation.rs) — sample_rotation (uses key array post-import)
- [crates/core/src/animation/player.rs](crates/core/src/animation/player.rs) — advance_time

## Completeness Checks

- [ ] **UNSAFE**: N/A
- [ ] **SIBLING**: If suspect 2 (B-spline decode), the same `deboor_cubic` is shared with translation and scale paths — verify those also produce sensible variation when authored. Translation we know works (#772 diagnostic showed mtidle's bip01/spine/pelvis with real per-frame deltas); scale is rarely animated and a regression there is silent.
- [ ] **DROP**: N/A
- [ ] **LOCK_ORDER**: AnimationPlayer is queried mutably and Transform is queried mutably in the same system — already TypeId-sorted. No new locks added by the investigation.
- [ ] **FFI**: N/A
- [ ] **TESTS**: Integration test loading `mtidle.kf`, advancing a synthetic AnimationPlayer over 1 second of dt, asserting at least one bone's local Transform diverges measurably from its frame-0 sample. Locks in regression on the rotation B-spline path that #772 didn't cover.
title: M41-IDLE: AnimationPlayer attaches but mtidle.kf produces no visible motion on spawned NPCs
